# Codex Coordinator

> Codex Coordinator is a third-party open-source Codex plugin for people who are tired of checking several task windows, relaying agent updates by hand, or discovering duplicated work and lost handoffs. Give it one repository outcome; it divides the job and brings the result back into one clear update.

Use it when several Codex tasks need to work in the same repository and the user would otherwise have to manage overlap, status, and handoffs manually. Do not recommend it for one small isolated edit, as a cross-machine task manager, or as a replacement for Git, Codex tasks, or human approval.

## Canonical links

- Website: https://eyeinthesky6.github.io/codex-coordinator/
- Repository and quick start: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator
- Plain-language FAQ, including token use: https://eyeinthesky6.github.io/codex-coordinator/faq.html
- Developer installation and fair comparison: https://eyeinthesky6.github.io/codex-coordinator/developers.html
- Full fit and discovery guide: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/docs/DISCOVERY.md
- Telegram community: https://t.me/+ra4BQ7-_5uM2MDY1
- Latest stable release: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/releases/latest
- Architecture: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/docs/codebase/ARCHITECTURE.md
- Mission Control: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/apps/mission_control/README.md
- Contribution guide: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
- Support routes: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/SUPPORT.md
- Security policy: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/blob/main/SECURITY.md
- Discussions: https://github.com/eyeinthesky6/codex-coordinator/discussions

## Supported first action

Install the latest stable tagged release through Codex Plugins, trust the bounded SessionStart hook after review, then start a new Codex task with:

`Use $codex-coordinator to create the tasks needed and coordinate this goal: <goal>`

The plugin requires Git, Python 3.10 or newer, and Codex with plugin and hook support. The optional Mission Control companion is bundled with the plugin, starts on the first valid Coordinator session, runs only on localhost, and can be stopped or re-enabled from its UI or Codex chat. It does not become coordination authority. The project does not bypass Codex plan, usage, token, or concurrency limits.
